entries.summary: There’s no end to the stealthy ways in which attackers develop and execute their tradecraft. In this case, it's as simple as hiding in plain sight.

entries.title: The Impact of Data Breaches on Our Society | Huntress
entries.summary: How do we calculate the total impact of a data breach? Visit the Huntress Blog to learn more about how cyber security awareness training can help to mitigate risk.

entries.title: How One Criminal Tried to Sell an MSP on the Dark Web | Huntress
entries.summary: In a rare encounter, we found ourselves directly interacting with a cybercriminal that took us down a dark web rabbit hole.

entries.title: Validating the SolarWinds N-central “Dumpster Diver” Vulnerability
entries.summary: Read more about Huntress validating the SolarWinds N-central “Dumpster Diver” Vulnerability.

entries.title: Validating the Bishop Fox Findings in ConnectWise Control | Huntress
entries.summary: The Huntress team validates eight vulnerabilities found in ConnectWise Control that ranged from low to high severity.

entries.title: Assisted Remediation in Action | Huntress
entries.summary: Learn how Huntress helped an MSP partner contain and remediate an Emotet/TrickBot infection with Assisted Remediation.

entries.title: Huntress Development Notes: Updating the Updater
entries.summary: If you’ve ever taken a look inside the Huntress Agent directory you may have noticed the file wyUpdate.exe.

entries.title: Keeping up with BlueKeep
entries.summary: Remote Desktop Services (RDS) benefit employees and IT administrators alike. With employees often working from anywhere, remote desktop reduces the physical burden of carrying a work laptop home 🏠. It also makes updating and managing systems easier, which can alleviate the administrative burden when handling a large network.

entries.title: Deep Dive: A LNK in the Chain
entries.summary: Read this blog to learn more about what the Huntress team discovered with LNK.

entries.title: Incident Education: Sales Ammo for the IT Arsenal
entries.summary: As a technical founder of a product startup, I’m as anti-FUD as it gets. However, the past three years have taught me how education can be a snake-oil free alternative.

entries.title: Rapid Response: ASUS Live Update Attack (Operation ShadowHammer)
entries.summary: Periodically, a large scale cybersecurity issue requires “all hands on deck” from the Huntress Team (see WannaCry, Kaseya Cryptominer, GANDGRAB outbreak). The unfolding ASUS Live Update fiasco also happens to be one of those moments. We’ve created this blog is to provide simple answers to a complex supply chain attack affecting global IT Departments.

entries.title: CVE-2017-18362: Arbitrary SQL Execution in ManagedITSync Integration
entries.summary: A vulnerability was discovered and disclosed in late 2017 that affected the ConnectWise ManagedITSync integration, designed to sync data between the ConnectWise Manage PSA and the Kaseya VSA RMM.

entries.title: Failing to Revive AUTOEXEC.BAT on Windows 7 & 10
entries.summary: Does AUTOEXEC.BAT still run on modern Windows? We test Windows 7/10, explore registry persistence, and revisit how attackers maintain access today.

entries.title: Distrusting Symantec Issued Certificates
entries.summary: Read more about security updates to the Huntress platform.

entries.title: Attackers Abuse Trust with Indirection
entries.summary: Preventive security products like antivirus have made major strides in their ability to detect malicious behaviors as opposed to weak/static signatures. When implemented properly, these heuristics are capable of discovering even the most cleverly obfuscated routines. But don’t ring the victory bells yet. This cat-and-mouse game is just getting started…

entries.title: Ask Huntress: Fake .XPS Invoice Leading to Credential Phishing
entries.summary: Explore this phishing campaign used the age-old “Please remit payment” spiel to lure potential victims into opening the attached file.

entries.title: Understanding Potentially Unwanted Programs Part I — It’s Not Always Malware
entries.summary: Regardless of your daily duties, we’ve all encountered annoying ads, unwanted pop-ups, or generically named rogue applications. These nuisances, commonly called Potentially Unwanted Programs (PUP), are often unknowingly installed when downloading free software.

entries.title: Deep Dive: Kaseya VSA Mining Payload
entries.summary: For many of us in the Managed Services Provider market, we were rocked with news of a vulnerability in Kaseya’s VSA product. The purpose of this blog is to shine technical light on what the Huntress ThreatOps team observed and analyzed thus far.

entries.title: Huntress Labs to Host Hands-On “Hacking Windows” Training for MSPs at IT Nation
entries.summary: IT Nation 2016 ended on a high note for Huntress Labs as we were named the “Best Newcomer” in the Partner Choice Awards. Now, as this year’s event quickly approaches, we are excited to continue our momentum.

entries.title: Abusing Trusted Applications with Nested Execution
entries.summary: Recently, my co-founders gave a talk at DerbyCon 7.0 on evading common persistence enumeration tools. Evasion using trusted applications has been a hot topic of discussion within the infosec community and is one of the techniques they covered in their presentation. However, very little discussion exists on why these matter or the steps researchers take to find “hosting” applications.

entries.title: MSP Moment: Worming Malware Brings New Complexities to the Threat Landscape
entries.summary: Over the past month, the Emotet family of malware has re-emerged as a formidable piece of crimeware, thanks to its new self-propagation techniques (undoubtedly inspired by the success of WannaCry and Petya).

entries.title: Redosdru — Encrypting DLL Payloads to Avoid On-Disk Signatures
entries.summary: Dive deep into Redosdru malware analysis, unpacking encrypted DLLs, keylogging behavior, and how Huntress defenders detect and respond.

entries.title: Deep Dive: Squashing an MSSQL Attack
entries.summary: During the previous MSP Moment, we walked our readers through an incident where our partner, NTConnections, used Huntress to discover a previously undetected breach. In this Deep Drive, we’ll examine the tradecraft used by the attackers to gain access through the database, kill/disable antivirus, download malicious files, and establish a persistent foothold within the network.

entries.title: Security Awareness Training Will Prevent Ransomware | Huntress
entries.summary: Ransomware security awareness training is the most effective way to prevent a ransomware attack against your organization and its employees. Read more in the Huntress Blog to find out how.

entries.title: MSP Moment: Squashing an MSSQL Attack
entries.summary: When it comes to breaches, it’s hard to find a silver lining when the end result is customer down time, data theft, or damaged reputations. For Managed Service Providers with tens to hundreds (or even thousands) of clients, the stakes are even higher. In this MSP Moment, we’re highlighting how NTConnections, a Washington DC based MSP, responded to a database outage which quickly escalated into an incident response effort.

entries.title: Troubleshooting Procmon & Sysmon v3.32
entries.summary: When it comes to troubleshooting software errors, analyzing malware samples, or discovering security misconfigurations, the Sysinternals Suite can likely solve your problem. For many IT and Security professionals, this collection of Windows utilities is similar to the Swiss Army knife.

entries.title: Huntress Wins ConnectWise IT Nation Partner’s Choice Award!
entries.summary: Huntress wins prestigious award at this year’s largest MSP industry conference, ConnectWise IT Nation, for industry-leading Managed Detection & Response service.

entries.title: How do you protect computers from attackers if you’re not familiar with hacking techniques?
entries.summary: How do you protect computers from attackers if you’re not familiar with hacking techniques? The historical answer to this question has been antivirus and firewalls. However, the last several years have demonstrated hackers can slip past these preventative technologies and cause devastating results to the victims.

entries.title: How My StubHub Account Got Hacked | Huntress
entries.summary: If you have a StubHub account, you are open to a major vulnerability of having your StubHub hacked. Learn more about how you are at risk & how Huntress' Security Awareness Training can help.
